test_scenarios: ["Attempt to bypass MFA for System Administrator and Finance Manager roles","Test session invalidation upon logout (server-side token revocation)","Attempt to reuse expired or invalidated registration/invitation tokens","Verify lockout mechanism activates after 5 failed login attempts","Test for concurrent session handling and idle timeout enforcement (15 min)"]
acceptance_criteria: Zero bypasses of MFA; Session tokens strictly invalidated; No account enumeration possible via error messages.
reporting_requirements: Auth vulnerability report linked to release ticket
trend_analysis: Track authentication failure rates and brute force attempt patterns

testing_tools: Stripe CLI, Custom C# Integration Tests
test_scenarios: ["Attempt to inject fraudulent 'payment_intent.succeeded' webhooks without valid Stripe signatures","Test replay attacks using previously processed webhook payloads","Attempt to initiate a refund for an amount greater than the original transaction","Verify vendor payout details are masked in API responses and logs","Attempt to trigger a payout for a project in a non-payable state (e.g., Active/Pending)"]
acceptance_criteria: All webhooks must verify signatures; Idempotency keys must prevent double-processing; Financial constraints enforced at domain level.
trend_analysis: Monitor rate of invalid webhook signatures

testing_tools: AWS Macie, Custom Python PII Scanners, Checkov
test_scenarios: ["Upload SOWs containing dummy PII (Names, SSN, Emails) and verify complete redaction before storage in 'sanitized-sow' bucket","Verify raw SOWs in S3 'raw-sow' bucket are encrypted with AWS KMS and not publicly accessible","Attempt to access raw SOWs via API without System Administrator role","Test GDPR 'Right to be Forgotten' workflow ensures PII is removed while retaining financial transaction records"]
acceptance_criteria: 100% PII redaction in sanitized bucket; Raw bucket access strictly limited to IAM role; Logs must not contain PII.
trend_analysis: Track PII detection confidence scores over time

compliance_requirement: OWASP API Security Top 10 (Broken Object Level Authorization)
test_scenarios: ["Vendor attempts to access Proposal data for a project they are not invited to (IDOR)","Client attempts to view internal Audit Logs","Finance Manager attempts to change System Admin configuration settings","Unauthenticated user attempts to access private API endpoints","Verify JWT scope validation for all microservices"]
acceptance_criteria: 403 Forbidden returned for all unauthorized cross-role or cross-tenant access attempts.

test_scenarios: ["Scan Terraform plan for open Security Groups (0.0.0.0/0 on non-public ports)","Verify EKS Control Plane logging is enabled","Check for hardcoded secrets in code or Terraform state","Verify S3 buckets enforce SSL-only access policies","Scan Docker images for critical CVEs before deployment"]
acceptance_criteria: Zero critical/high config violations; All images scanned and clean of critical CVEs.
trend_analysis: Track mean time to remediation for infra vulnerabilities

test_scenarios: ["Upload malware-infected PDF/DOCX to SOW upload endpoint","Attempt Path Traversal attacks via filenames (e.g., '../../etc/passwd')","Upload executable files disguised as PDF (MIME type spoofing)","Upload files exceeding 10MB limit to test DoS resilience"]
acceptance_criteria: Malware detected and rejected; Path traversal sanitized; Only allowed MIME types accepted.
